query: DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak June 2026 death toll trajectory cases

# DRC Ebola Bundibugyo Outbreak – June 2026: Research Findings

## Overview

The Democratic Republic of the Congo is currently experiencing its **17th Ebola outbreak**, caused by the **Bundibugyo ebolavirus (BDBV)** — a strain for which **no approved vaccine or treatment** currently exists. The outbreak was officially declared on **15 May 2026** and was designated a **Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC)** by WHO on **16–17 May 2026**, just two days after the declaration. The outbreak has also spread to **Uganda** via cross-border transmission.

## Case and Death Trajectory (Chronological)

The outbreak has grown extremely rapidly. Key data points from official sources:

| Date | Confirmed Cases (DRC) | Confirmed Deaths (DRC) | CFR (DRC) | Uganda Cases | Source |
|------|----------------------|----------------------|-----------|-------------|--------|
| 5 May 2026 | Initial alert (unknown illness, 4 HCW deaths in Mongbwalu) | — | — | — | WHO Sitrep 01 |
| 15 May 2026 | Outbreak declared (lab confirmation of BDBV) | — | — | 2 imported | WHO Sitrep 01 |
| 18 May 2026 | (WHO Sitrep 01 published – exact case count not in abstract) | — | — | — | WHO Sitrep 01 |
| 19 May 2026 | **33** | — | — | — | ReliefWeb community engagement report |
| 30 May 2026 | **282** | — | — | 5 (incl. 1 death) | ReliefWeb report; WHO risk assessment |
| 31 May 2026 | **291** | **43** | ~14.8% | — | Project HOPE Sitrep #3 |
| 2 June 2026 | **321** | **48** | ~14.9% | — | DRC Ministry of Health via OCHA |
| 6 June 2026 | **515** | — | — | — | Mongbwalu epi report (baseline) |
| 10 June 2026 | **676** | **136** | **20.1%** | 19 (incl. 2 deaths) | WHO DON607 (13 June) |
| 13 June 2026 | **782** | **181** | **23.1%** | — | Mongbwalu epi trends report (16 June); Save the Children (15 June) |

### Growth Rate
- **May 19 → May 30**: 33 → 282 cases = **~8.5-fold increase in 11 days**
- **June 6 → June 13**: 515 → 782 cases = **+267 cases (+51.8%) in one week**
- **Overall first month (May 15 → June 15)**: 0 → 782 confirmed cases, 181 confirmed deaths

## Geographic Spread

- **Epicenter**: Ituri Province (~91.6% of confirmed cases as of 13 June)
  - Main hotspots: **Bunia** (185 cases), **Rwampara** (137 cases), **Mongbwalu** (132 cases), **Nyankunde** (33 cases) — as of 10 June
- **Total health zones affected**: **31** across three provinces (Ituri: 19/36 HZ; North Kivu: 9/35 HZ; South Kivu: 1/34 HZ) — as of 13 June
- **Uganda**: 19 confirmed cases (14 imported, 5 secondary), 2 deaths — from Kampala and Wakiso districts. No new cases for 6 days as of 11 June. No documented community transmission.
- **Regional preparedness**: Burundi, Rwanda, South Sudan (Priority 1); Angola, CAR, Ethiopia, Kenya, Congo, Tanzania, Zambia (Priority 2)

## Key Contextual Factors

- **No approved vaccine or treatment** for Bundibugyo ebolavirus exists. Clinical trials are being organized for **MBP134** and **REGN3479** (treatment) and **obeldesivir** (post-exposure prophylaxis).
- **Third-largest Ebola outbreak ever recorded in DRC** (Save the Children, 15 June).
- **IASC System-Wide Scale-Up** activated on 7 June for initial 3-month period.
- **159 health zones** categorized as affected or at risk (WHO, 8 June).
- **5,768 contacts** under follow-up as of 10 June (4,703 in Ituri, 841 in North Kivu, 224 in South Kivu). Follow-up rate: ~72%.
- **16 healthcare worker infections** confirmed as of 10 June.
- **Children disproportionately affected**: 52 children infected (including 16 toddlers/infants), 19 child deaths. Children under 14 have **more than twice** the case fatality rate of patients aged 15–44.
- **Water/sanitation crisis**: Only 20% of people in Mongbwalu have access to clean water; only 1 in 5 health facilities in Ituri has adequate clean water access (Oxfam).
- **Outbreak likely underestimated** per Oxfam; MSF warns most patients arrive at treatment centers at a late stage and were never identified as contacts.
- **Complex conflict environment**: Armed conflict in Ituri, displaced populations, and security incidents at health facilities hinder response.

## Response Status

- WHO risk assessment: **Very high** for DRC, **high** for Uganda and bordering countries, **low** for rest of Africa and globally.
- Response described as "outpacing the response effort" (MSF, 15 June). Gaps in surveillance, diagnosis, contact tracing, and community engagement persist.
- Africa CDC and WHO launched a joint continental response plan on 5 June.
- Logistics Cluster established transport corridors from Entebbe to Bunia.

16 healthcare worker infections confirmed as of 10 June 2026
5,768 contacts under follow-up with ~72% follow-up rate as of 10 June
52 children infected including 16 toddlers/infants; 19 child deaths; children under 14 have more than twice the CFR of patients aged 15-44
Only 20% of people in Mongbwalu (population ~140,000) have access to clean water (Oxfam)
IASC System-Wide Scale-Up activated on 7 June 2026 for initial 3-month period
Clinical trials being organized for MBP134 and REGN3479 (treatment) and obeldesivir (post-exposure prophylaxis)
MSF warns outbreak is 'outpacing the response effort'; Oxfam says true scale is likely underestimated
WHO risk assessment: Very High for DRC, High for Uganda and bordering countries, Low globally
